channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US assures no hindrance to Venezuela opposition leader's return, seeks conditions for safe return
item.description: The United States has stated it will not block Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado's return to her country, though officials emphasized the need for proper conditions to ensure her safet...

item.title: Quebec warns of rising cyclosporiasis cases linked to contaminated food and water
item.description: Quebec health officials have confirmed an uptick in cyclosporiasis cases, a parasitic infection that causes severe diarrhea and dehydration. The illness, caused by the Cyclospora parasite, is typicall...

item.title: Family of Woman Found Dead at Minister's Residence Refuses to Comment on Death Circumstances
item.description: Mary Habila, a 26-year-old physiotherapist, was found dead at the residence of Nigeria's Minister of Works, David Umahi, on June 27, 2026. Her father, Baba Habila, has refused to comment on the circum...

item.title: Africa's Climate Resilience Lessons from the Super El Niño Crisis
item.description: The article discusses the impending 'super El Niño' event and its potential impact on Africa, emphasizing the continent's resilience strategies. It highlights five key lessons: the importance of prepa...

item.title: BARMM Election Period Begins with Gun Ban Ahead of Parliamentary Polls
item.description: The Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) officially launched its first parliamentary elections on July 16, 2026, marking the start of a strict gun ban that will last until September...

item.title: Meta's NameTag Face Recognition Technology: Existence and Legal Implications
item.description: The article explores Meta's development of a face recognition system called NameTag, which the company has designed and deployed on millions of phones. It highlights the conflicting statements from Me...

item.title: Pakistan's Crypto Regulator Seeks Clarification on Shariah-Compliant Digital Assets
item.description: The Pakistan Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (PVARA) chairman, Bilal bin Saqib, has requested Jamia Darul Uloom to differentiate between speculative cryptocurrencies and asset-backed digital token...

item.title: Air Quality Alert Issued Across Region Due to Wildfire Smoke
item.description: The Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC) has issued an air quality alert across multiple regions, urging residents to limit outdoor activities due to wildfire smoke affecting the area. The ale...

item.title: Channel 9 reporter Danika Mason opens up about viral slurred Winter Olympics broadcast and its aftermath
item.description: Channel 9 sports reporter Danika Mason has spoken candidly about the challenging aftermath of a viral live cross from the Winter Olympics in Italy that saw her slur her words on air. During a segment ...

item.title: Richemont reports strong Q1 results with jewelry and watch divisions leading growth
item.description: Richemont, the Switzerland-based luxury group controlled by the Rupert family, reported a strong performance for the first quarter of its 2027 financial year. Sales rose 20% at constant exchange rates...

item.title: FIFA Compensation for Manchester United Following Manuel Ugarte's Injury
item.description: Manuel Ugarte, Uruguay's midfielder, suffered a severe knee ligament injury during the 2026 World Cup, leaving him sidelined for an extended period. FIFA has activated its Club Protection Programme to...

item.title: Climate-Vulnerable Nations Face Delays in Receiving Loss and Damage Funds
item.description: The Fund for Responding to Loss and Damage (FRLD) board has postponed funding decisions until December 2026, citing an overwhelming number of proposals and limited financial resources. Nearly $500m is...

item.title: Trump Announces 'Patriot Games' Youth Competition in D.C.
item.description: The article discusses U.S. President Donald Trump's planned 'Patriot Games' event, a youth competition organized as part of his D.C. celebrations for the nation's 250th anniversary. Political correspo...

item.title: Prince William and Kate Middleton announce engagement
item.description: Prince William and Kate Middleton have officially announced their engagement, marking a significant milestone in their relationship. The couple, who have been together since 2001, have faced public sc...

item.title: GOP Strategist Criticizes Jay Clayton's Confirmation Hearing Performance and Preparedness
item.description: Jay Clayton, President Donald Trump's nominee to serve as Director of National Intelligence, faced criticism following his Senate Intelligence Committee confirmation hearing after struggling to answer...

item.title: FCC Chair's Conflict of Interest in Media Merger Raises Ethical Concerns
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) faces scrutiny over potential conflicts of interest involving its chair, Ajit Pai, amid a high-profile media merger between Paramount and Skydance Media. Th...

item.title: Gen Z Resists AI Adoption Amid Generational Divide in Tech Use
item.description: The article explores the generational divide in attitudes toward artificial intelligence (AI), highlighting how Gen Z is pushing back against its adoption. While older generations like baby boomers vi...

item.title: PEI Liberal leader steps down after by-election defeat
item.description: Prince Edward Island Liberal leader Robert Mitchell has resigned following his party's second consecutive by-election loss. The decision comes after Mitchell placed second in the Cornwall-Meadowbank r...

item.title: NAACAM Calls for 60% Local Content in South African Automotive Manufacturing to Boost Economy
item.description: The National Association of Automotive Component and Allied Manufacturers (NAACAM) is pushing for the South African government to mandate a 60% local content requirement in automotive manufacturing to...

item.title: Delhi court allows Umar Khalid to continue two supervised weekly video meetings with his family
item.description: A Delhi court has allowed activist Umar Khalid to continue having two supervised video meetings, or e-mulakats, with his family every week while he remains in judicial custody. The order was passed by...
